---
title: "How to Write and Submit Your Post"
subtitle: "A step-by-step guide for PS51009D students"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

This guide covers everything you need to write your science communication post and submit it. You do not need to install anything — all the tools are available in your browser via RStudio Server.

::: {.callout-note}
## Two separate things: submission and publication
**Submitting for marking** happens via [Moodle](https://learn.gold.ac.uk/mod/coursework/view.php?id=1794744) — this is mandatory.

**Publishing on the blog** is optional. If you want your post published here, upload your files to the [PS51009D OneDrive folder](https://goldsmithscollege-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/gwrig002_campus_goldsmiths_ac_uk/IgARGfnH7ojgRqhRM7PF7HR_AbRQbdcJ_jeRtykPpkMi2E8?e=4m1SIt) and Gordon will add it to the site.
:::

---

## Step 1 — Open RStudio Server

Go to the Goldsmiths RStudio Server in your browser and log in with your Goldsmiths credentials. You do not need to install R or RStudio on your own computer.

---

## Step 2 — Create a new Quarto Blog project

1. Click **File → New Project**
2. Select **New Directory**
3. Select **Quarto Blog**
4. Give your project a name — use your surname and topic, e.g. `wright-meditation`
5. Click **Create Project**

RStudio creates a ready-to-use blog structure for you:

```
_quarto.yml          ← site settings (you do not need to edit this)
index.qmd            ← the listing page (you do not need to edit this)
about.qmd            ← an about page (optional)
posts/               ← your post goes in here
styles.css           ← styling (you do not need to edit this)
```

---

## Step 3 — Create your post

1. Inside the `posts/` folder, create a new subfolder named with your surname and topic — e.g. `wright-meditation`
2. Inside that subfolder, create a new file called `index.qmd`

Your structure should look like this:

```
posts/
  wright-meditation/
    index.qmd        ← your post
    images/          ← create this folder for your visual aids
    references.bib   ← your reference list (if using .bib citations)
```

---

## Step 4 — Write your post

Open `index.qmd` and start with this YAML header at the very top:

```yaml
---
title: "The title of your post"
author: "Your Name"
date: "2025-04-15"
description: "One sentence that will appear in the listing."
categories: [human-AI interaction]
image: "images/cover.jpg"
bibliography: references.bib
---
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## Date format
Use `YYYY-MM-DD` format exactly. Other formats will cause errors.
:::

Choose your category from the module topics: `human-AI interaction`, `meditation`, `individual differences`, `neuropsychology`, `music psychology`, `expert performance`, `eyewitness testimony`, `stalking`.

### What your post must include

- 800–1000 words written for a non-expert audience (third person)
- Covers the research question, method, findings, and conclusions
- At least one **original visual aid** you created — not taken from the article
- In-text citations and a full reference list in APA style

### Adding your visual aid

Save your image in the `images/` subfolder and include it like this:

```markdown
![A clear caption describing what the figure shows.](images/your-figure.png)
```

Your visual aid can be a diagram, infographic, chart, or table — anything you created yourself. Canva, PowerPoint, and Excel are all fine.

### Citations and references

Add `bibliography: references.bib` to your YAML header. Then cite in text:

```markdown
The study found that meditation increased compassionate behaviour [@condon2013].
```

At the end of your post, add:

```markdown
## References

::: {#refs}
:::
```

Quarto formats the reference list in APA style automatically.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Getting .bib entries
In Zotero: select your item → right-click → Export Item → BibTeX → save as `references.bib`.

In Google Scholar: click Cite under a result → BibTeX → copy the entry into your `references.bib` file.
:::

---

## Step 5 — Preview your post

Click the **Render** button (at the top of the editor) to build the site and preview it in the browser. Check:

- Your text appears correctly
- Your image displays
- Citations and reference list appear at the end
- No error messages in the console

---

## Step 6 — Submit your work

### For marking (required)

Download the rendered HTML file and submit it via Moodle:

1. After rendering, find the `_site/posts/your-folder-name/index.html` file in the Files pane
2. Tick the checkbox next to it → click **More → Export** to download it
3. Submit the downloaded HTML file via **[Moodle](https://learn.gold.ac.uk/mod/coursework/view.php?id=1794744)**

Your reflection piece goes to the same Moodle submission page on the same deadline: **Monday 11 May, 12 noon**.

### For publication on the blog (optional)

If you want your post published here:

1. In the Files pane, tick your entire post folder (e.g. `posts/wright-meditation/`)
2. Click **More → Export** to download it as a zip file
3. Upload the zip to the **[PS51009D OneDrive folder](https://goldsmithscollege-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/gwrig002_campus_goldsmiths_ac_uk/IgARGfnH7ojgRqhRM7PF7HR_AbRQbdcJ_jeRtykPpkMi2E8?e=4m1SIt)**

Gordon will review and add it to the site. You will be notified when it is live.

---

## Optional: make your post more engaging

These are not required for the assessment, but are available if you want to go further.

### Callout boxes

```markdown
::: {.callout-note}
## Key concept
Define a term or highlight an important idea here.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Limitation
Flag a methodological caveat here.
:::
```

Types: `callout-note` (blue), `callout-tip` (green), `callout-warning` (orange), `callout-important` (red).

### Pull quote

```markdown
::: {.pullquote}
"Your most important finding in one sentence."
:::
```

### Tabset panels

```markdown
::: {.panel-tabset}

## Method
How the study was run.

## Findings
What they found.

## Implications
What it means.

:::
```

---

## Before you submit — checklist

- [ ] 800–1000 words, written in the third person
- [ ] Covers research question, method, findings, and conclusions
- [ ] At least one original visual aid you created
- [ ] In-text citations throughout
- [ ] Full APA reference list at the end
- [ ] Reflection piece complete (480–600 words)
- [ ] Both components submitted via [Moodle](https://learn.gold.ac.uk/mod/coursework/view.php?id=1794744) by **Monday 11 May, 12 noon**

---

## Getting help

See the [Example post](posts/example-post/index.qmd) for a complete worked example, and the [Formatting guide](posts/formatting-guide/index.qmd) for copy-paste code for every feature.

For questions about the assessment, post on the Moodle forum so everyone benefits from the answer.
